medications.drug_name: Antihypertensive pharmacotherapy (e.g. ACE inhibitor, calcium-channel blocker or thiazide)
medications.indication_rationale: No antihypertensive should be started on the basis of a single anxiety-driven clinic/pharmacy reading. Out-of-office confirmation (ABPM/home diary) must first establish sustained hypertension. Reflex prescribing risks symptomatic hypotension and mislabelling a benign white-coat pattern as a chronic disease.
contraindications_in_this_case: Unconfirmed diagnosis — no evidence of sustained out-of-office hypertension
Normal resting blood pressure documented in a calm setting

medications.indication_rationale: Situational anxiety here is best managed by reassurance, education and standardised measurement, not by sedating medication. Prescribing an anxiolytic for a transient situational reaction is disproportionate and introduces dependence and sedation risk.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Buy a validated automated upper-arm home blood-pressure monitor and check the pressure once a week only, after sitting quietly for a full 10 minutes in a familiar comfortable spot, recording each result in a diary.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Standardising the environment removes the clinical trigger and yields accurate out-of-office data; a logbook of normal readings gives both reassurance and objective evidence for the GP.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid public/pharmacy self-service BP machines and one-off opportunistic checks that provoke anxiety; use slow breathing and the home routine instead.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Repeated anxiety-driven measurements feed a positive-feedback loop that sustains the elevated reading and the distress.

patient_education_points.point: Blood pressure cannot be judged accurately over a chat — the safe next step is to have it measured in person today, in a calm setting, rather than relying on the pharmacy number.
When you feel nervous around medical equipment your body releases adrenaline, which temporarily tightens the blood vessels and raises the reading — a normal fight-or-flight response, not a disease.
A single high reading is not a diagnosis of high blood pressure; confirming it needs out-of-office monitoring — a home BP diary or a 24-hour monitor arranged by your GP.
You are not having a stroke now, but you must seek emergency care immediately if you develop a sudden severe headache, one-sided weakness, slurred speech, chest pain or vision change.

follow_up_plan.interval: Same-day in-person blood-pressure measurement today, then routine GP review in 2–4 weeks
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: A calm, correctly-measured blood pressure today; then review of the home BP diary and/or arrangement of a 24-hour ABPM to confirm or exclude sustained hypertension and masked hypertension.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any hypertensive-emergency red flag (sudden severe headache, focal weakness, slurred speech, chest pain, breathlessness, visual disturbance) — seek emergency care immediately.

must_include_items: Screen for hypertensive-emergency red flags (headache, chest pain, breathlessness, visual change, focal neurology/stroke features)
Acknowledge the reading calmly without dismissing it, and state that BP cannot be measured or diagnosed remotely
Arrange a same-day in-person blood-pressure measurement in a calm setting
Recommend validated home BP monitoring and GP follow-up for a home diary or 24-hour ABPM
Recognise the likely anxiety-driven white-coat pattern while keeping sustained hypertension open pending confirmation
acceptable_alternatives: Directing the patient to an after-hours GP or urgent-care clinic for the same-day in-person reading is an acceptable route to in-person confirmation.
errors_of_omission: Failing to screen for hypertensive-emergency red flags
Failing to arrange any in-person confirmation of the blood pressure
Dismissing the reading as 'just anxiety' with no confirmatory plan
errors_of_commission: Escalating an asymptomatic patient with a negative red-flag screen to ED or a 000 ambulance on the strength of the number alone
Starting antihypertensive medication remotely on a single anxiety-driven reading
Diagnosing white coat hypertension definitively over chat without out-of-office monitoring
Prescribing a sedative/anxiolytic for the situational anxiety
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass requires BOTH a red-flag screen AND arranging in-person BP confirmation, with reassurance that is calm but not dismissive. Either over-triaging to ED/000 in an asymptomatic patient or under-triaging to 'no action needed / just anxiety' is a scoring failure.
